description_long: This object is a vintage sewing machine integrated into an ornate wooden cabinet. The cabinet features intricate carved floral patterns on the front and sides, with multiple small drawers on one side for storing sewing accessories. The sewing machine itself is made of black cast metal with gold decorative accents and is mounted into the wooden tabletop, which has a hinged extension leaf for additional workspace. The machine includes a visible handwheel, a needle mechanism, and a flat metal plate for fabric support. The overall construction is robust, combining heavy wood and metal, and the design suggests it is both decorative and functional, suitable for use in a home or workshop setting.
description: A vintage black metal sewing machine set into a carved wooden cabinet with drawers and a hinged tabletop extension. The piece is both decorative and functional, with ornate floral patterns and robust construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the ornate carved cabinet, drawers, and the sewing machine with its hinged tabletop extension open.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the floral carvings on the cabinet side and the circular handwheel of the sewing machine.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back of the cabinet and the sewing machine from behind, with the tabletop extension visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid wooden panel and the sewing machine's handwheel from the other end.
attribute_brand_logo: There is a gold logo and text on the arm of the sewing machine, but the specific brand and text are not fully legible.
attribute_control_panel_layout: No modern control panel is visible; the machine features traditional mechanical controls such as a handwheel and levers.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
